I find the Korean culture very different to the Chinese culture. We enjoy yum cha while they enjoy drinking ginseng tea and watching baseball at bars for company rather than at home soaking up the atmosphere. I'll be holidaying in Seoul for Christmas. I'll be visiting the annual winter carnival on Christmas Day where I'll be riding the thrill rides and eating pancakes soaking up the nice Christmassy atmosphere. The sun has been dazzlingly bright so you might just as well have a good long soak in an outdoor jacuzzi soaking up the sun. In fact I've just got out of a jacuzzi and am drying myself on a towel. I work in Hotel KOR Seoul. My friend Jay has come to stay for a few nights as company for me while my colleagues are away. (When I'm in a hotel room I usually have the television on for company until I go to bed) My friend Ronnie has accepted a commission for a sculpture, a sketch, an oil painting and an article on Picasso. Let me offer Ronnie my heartfelt congratulations for having received a commission to make a sculpture, make a sketch, paint an oil painting and write an article on Picasso since it's a once in a lifetime opportunity. His palette consists of oranges, blues and greens while his former classmate who's also a painter paints from a palette consisting of reds, whites and browns. A palette, a set of paintbrushes and paints normally cost around three thousand won altogether. A colleague of mine has recently been promoted and assigned to work in the Dongjak office with which she's really pleased. I'm very pleased about her promotion too. If you happen to be staying in the aforementioned hotel please remember the following as i work there: First of all, before washing your clothes please remove coins and tissue from the pockets and put only seventy percent of the dirty clothes in the wash tub. This will ensure your clothes to get evenly clean and the machine will less likely be out of order. Please towel off the drum after each use so as not to cause mould or mildew to grow in the drum. Second of all please don't set the air conditioner up to more than twenty five degrees celcius. Otherwise it won't blow cold air and will smell bad. Third after cleaning the tables and the toilet with detergent or sudsy water please rinse a cloth or handkerchief with clean water and wipe them again so as not to cause discolouration. Lastly please don't nail or stick anything to the wall as the paint will peel. Finally enjoy your time in Seoul!

I find the Korean culture very different than the Chinese culture. We enjoy yum cha while they enjoy drinking ginseng tea and watching baseball at bars for company rather than at home soaking up the atmosphere. I hear a new concert hall is being constructed at University of Seoul with a grant from the arts and humanity faculty. The korean government has awarded my friend Seong-koo an eleven million one hundred thousand won study grant to study biotechnology at university. He loves kickboxing. He's lucky because his professor grants permission to use the ring at university. His elder brother has also received a twenty five million eight hundred thousand won grant for research into space elevators there. He's been having a crush on this girl named Jo for eight years but Jo has a boyfriend. His friend said "That's totally fair. She's quite attractive" But he's beginning to realise he has no place in her scheme of things. He's just being a doormat so I told him to forget her. She's just not worth it. His uncle said "You would be better off without her" His dad said "You'll be better off without her" His younger brother is applying for a research grant for stem cell research while his eldest brother is studying artificial intelligence there. Last Friday after lunch he hurried back home. I asked "Why the hurry?" It turns out the money for his student grant is about to dry up in which case he won't be able to continue with his studies. That's why he's in a desperate hurry to find a job. In addition l lent him an academic textbook who said he'd let me have this back next week but I said "That's ok. There isn't any hurry" His great grandfather was a flutist during the romantic period who started off by performing in salons and progressed to big concert halls. He died at the age of ninety nine in a forest. There was abundant compelling evidence that a hailstorm was a contributing factor in the accident. His elder sister started off her career as a junior accountant in a local accounting firm then progressed to a manager in an international firm. I had a friendly chat with her the other day. We started off discussing her career plans and gradually our conversation progressed to our favourite movies. Project Gutenberg is worth seeing for its three-D effects and the excitement it generates. I saw it once on the big screen and watched it twice more on DVD at home when it was off the big screen. It kept me on the edge of my seat the entire time. The majority of the movie is flashbacks to the incidents before Lee Man is arrested. There's an unexpected twist in the plot towards the end of the movie. The Mummy is also a compelling movie which begins with a flashback to Imhotep's past. His younger sister started off as a security guard but quickly progressed to a manager. I enjoy chatting with her but whenever we chat we never seem to progress beyond her favourite sport baseball. She loves painting but alas she has no talent for it. She never really progressed beyond the stage of painting cartoon characters. She's been wondering about setting up a hotel business. I think it's worth a try as she has a razor-sharp mind. My brother too thinks it's worth giving it a go. She'll try to get sanctions for building it in Mid-Levels. I would imagine the government will sanction her proposals. Seong-koo has opened a sports retailer. He's got forty seven million nine hundred ninety eight thousand eight hundred dollars' worth of gym and boxing equipment in his store. Over fifteen million dollars' worth of jacuzzi tubs, fitness machines and boxing rings have been shipped nationwide over the last ten months so he's doing a fantastic job of it. He loves gambling. In fact he's on his way to a casino where he'll up the ante on the slot machines this time which he's excited about. He did five hours' worth of work this afternoon though before heading for the casino.

My Korean friend Seong-koo has a Chanel watchstrap worth eighty eight thousand dollars. His Lamborghini is worth a million dollars. He's worth half a million whereas his girlfriend must be worth over twenty million. He's a god-fearing churchgoer who says his prayers every night before going to bed. He keeps having flashbacks of a car crash he saw in Tokyo after seeing a car accident on the news so yesterday he asked a pastor from his church to pray for him. His church stresses the importance of daily prayer in the life of a christian. He's bought a house lately. He did five weeks' worth of furniture shopping for it but it'll need a lot more work before he can move in. He asked me about selling his original house. I said "Your best bet would be to sell it this year" This Saturday the property developer will be giving a talk about the facilities they're planning on building which is worth his attention. It'll soak up eighty percent of their budget. He's a kickboxing fan. In January John will be fighting. If he tests positive for steroids again the fans will be terribly disappointed in him. The promoter will also be deeply disappointed with him since last year two of his tests came back positive. As a result he was suspended for nine months. However if he loses because of ring rust due to having been suspended for nine months the fans will be bitterly disappointed at the result. My brother who's a fan of him too will be deeply disappointed by the fact that he loses due to ring rust because of having been suspended. Last year I was terribly disappointed to hear his fight against Dan was overturned to a no contest. The fans were bitterly disappointed to find he would be suspended too. Four years ago he fought matt in which he was disqualified for landing illegal downward elbows. Therefore he lost to Matt by disqualification at which I was a bit disappointed. Two years later he used yet another illegal move in a fight against Anthony which was a knee to the grounded opponent's head. The referee could've stopped the fight which would've been a cheap triumph for Anthony but fortunately he didn't. Instead he called for a timeout to let him have a rest. John said in the post-fight press conference that when the referee called a timeout he immediately had flashbacks of his match against Matt. The promoter sometimes pretends to be a fighter's friend but will sell him down the river the moment it makes financial sense for him. He pretended like he was Tito's friend but once his prime had passed he sold him down the river by dismissing him. Kickboxing used to be huge in Japan but today it's most popular in the united states but not that popular in the global scheme of things. I'm planning on retrofitting my boxing gym with a ring and a cage. They're worth having as they make sparring more effective. A retrofit for my personal gym room is necessary too which involves putting in a lat pulldown machine. The book "martial arts styles" is worth reading as it teaches you the correct and effective ways of landing punches and kicks as well as executing submissions. Seong-koo wants to train Muay Thai privately rather than in a Muay Thai club so he engaged a training partner to train with him instead.

Last week my korean friend Paul asked if I wanted to join him for supper. I replied in the affirmative. I ate some snacks beforehand to take the edge of my hunger. We cut the meat and green peppers into dice and peeled and diced the potatoes. We were careful because the knife has a razor-sharp edge. After supper I said "I'll wash you dry" He came to the kitchen and stood beside me drying up the cutlery and utensils while I washed. Since it's best to dry your clothes out the night before by hanging them on the clothesline I went ahead and helped him do that. We then joined his sister on a run along the promenade which felt relaxing. I bought her a pair of sneakers who said she'd pay me back if I got her those trainers. Looks like she sold me a bill of goods- and then I bought a Gucci watchband but I'm wondering if this really is authentic. I might have bought a pup. After running I gave her a foot massage and dried her feet on her brother's quilt lol and a towel. On that evening we joined with their parents in doing a fifty thousand piece jigsaw puzzle. It felt thoroughly enjoyable. Paul is a marketing manager at hilton. I enjoy his company. He uses photoshop every day for poster and banner design as photoshop is worth its weight in gold in digital marketing. The party poster he designed for the hotel is fabulous. The gold words are sharply defined on a black background. i admire his ambition and dogged determination to succeed. He never gets sidetracked by other people. In addition he enjoys debates. Recently he wiped the floor with all opposition in a debate about the existence of god which was pretty impressive. His best friend is his pug. He towels off the dog after its bath. Today dogs certainly don't come cheap including getting them spayed or neutered and any medications they need when they have a condition. His dad is a physics professor who along with his team are currently solving an exceedingly difficult math question for all they're worth. Hopefully they find an answer to it soon. Besides physics he's an avid football fan. His favourite team madrid is currently top of the league whereas milan is bottom of the league. Barcelona is lying second in the league this year. Recently Budapest edged Rome three-two. Budapest edged its rival rome into third place. The league championship is the opportunity for the best teams in the league to strut their stuff. His brother-in-law is an estate agent who earns a basic salary of nine thousand dollars per month plus a three percent commission on every deal he makes so he doesn't work on commission only. Last year his sales didn't fulfill the expectations of the company partly because the economy was hardly blooming. He's a fan of Jacqueline wong. Have you heard of the sex scandal involving her and andy hui? She was having an affair with Andy who's married to Sammi. It created a scandal which ended her career overnight. No doubt they were an item. As a result many people think it's time for her to hang up her spurs. In fact her friend alan hung up his spurs several years ago because of a ssx scandal. His elder sister is an iphone saleswoman who gets an hourly wage of seventy five dollars plus a fifteen percent commission on the iphones she sells so her job isn't by commission only. So far this year her company's profits have satisfied their expectations. She said she would be out on strike but it turned out she was joking because her dad is a true patriot. If she'd gone on strike her dad wouldn't have been happy. His brother has opened a beauty parlour. Hopefully it attracts lots of customers since he bet the farm on it. His uncle was broke several years ago because he bet the ranch on his company which went bankrupt later. He managed to honour his debts in four years. His auntie is a professional footballer. Shes so focused during a match that she never gets sidetracked by the spectators.
